claim: Context farmers (scheduled agents running 24/7) can automatically ingest new content from sources and update Karpathy-style wikis without manual intervention
actionable: Set up scheduled agents to periodically fetch from MCP-connected sources (YouTube, Slack, etc.) and auto-ingest into markdown wikis, synced to GitHub
source_title: Build Your Own Self Improving AI Wiki in 11 Minutes (Free Skill)

claim: The key insight separating taste from fetching: humans select trustworthy sources (taste), but fetching new content from those sources is automatable (a cron job)
actionable: When building content systems, have humans curate source selection once, then automate continuous fetching from those sources using scheduled agents
source_title: Build Your Own Self Improving AI Wiki in 11 Minutes (Free Skill)

claim: Wikis synced to GitHub with auto-commit on updates allow distributed teams to pull fresh context while keeping persistent storage decoupled from laptop availability
actionable: Store wikis in private GitHub repos with auto-commit/push on updates, allowing org-wide access and scheduled farmers to run in cloud without local machine dependency
source_title: Build Your Own Self Improving AI Wiki in 11 Minutes (Free Skill)

claim: Any MCP connector (YouTube, Slack, Fireflies, Notion) can become a context source for a wiki farmer, enabling automated knowledge bases across business context
actionable: Map available MCP connectors to knowledge areas (meetings, code, messages, content) and set up farmers for each, building multi-layered context systems
source_title: Build Your Own Self Improving AI Wiki in 11 Minutes (Free Skill)

claim: Scheduled agents in Claude Code can run on Anthropic infrastructure with built-in GitHub clone and MCP access, removing need for local machine to be on
actionable: Use Claude Code scheduled agents for 24/7 context farming: set time, pass farm prompt, agent clones repo, runs MCP connections, commits changes autonomously
source_title: Build Your Own Self Improving AI Wiki in 11 Minutes (Free Skill)

claim: Removing human ingestion as bottleneck to context layer (automating fetching, not curation) lets wikis grow exponentially and compound user knowledge without overhead
actionable: Design context systems where humans touch them once (source selection) and automated agents handle continuous growth, enabling knowledge compound without maintenance burden
source_title: Build Your Own Self Improving AI Wiki in 11 Minutes (Free Skill)
